answer:
**Iqbal's "Khitab ba Jawanan-e-Islam" (Bang-E-Dara Part 3, ch. 13)** invokes the heroic memory of Muslim forebears—desert Arabs who toppled empires through action and proud poverty—to shame contemporary youth into revival.[^1] Its most demanding line indicts their inertia: "You can have no true kinship with your forefathers, for you are all talk, they were all action; you are static, they were ever-moving."[^2]

**Manu’s Book 2 (Manusmṛti, ch. 2)** codifies the brahmachārin's (celibate student's) regimen of ritual purity, guru-service, Vedic study, alms-begging, and sense-restraint within varṇāśrama norms.[^3] Its most demanding line mandates total sexual continence: "He should sleep alone everywhere and should never spill his seed; for by spilling his seed from desire, he breaks his vow."[^4]

Iqbal's action-heroic pedagogy—urging world-seizing khalīfa-style dynamism—presupposes a different polity to land than Manu's ritual-hierarchical one. The former requires an expansionist Islamic ummah of mobile conquerors (e.g., "World-seizers and world-rulers"),[^5] reviving against colonial stasis; the latter, a sedentary varṇa kingdom of Brahmin supremacy in Āryāvarta, with fixed castes and guru-disciple castes.[^6] Manu's brahmachārin trains for ritual eternity in a birth-based order; Iqbal's youth, for temporal resurgence in a borderless faith-community.

[^1]: [*Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 13 (translation), [chapter](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/13-khitab-ba-jawanan-e-islam/translation/)](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/13-khitab-ba-jawanan-e-islam/translation/).

[^2]: [*Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 13 (translation), [p-365462](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/13-khitab-ba-jawanan-e-islam/translation/#p-365462).

[^3]: [*Manusmṛti*, ch. 2 (translation), [chapter](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/)](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/).

[^4]: [*Manusmṛti*, ch. 2 (translation), [p-51cabf](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/#p-51cabf).

[^5]: [*Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 13 (translation), [p-365462](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/13-khitab-ba-jawanan-e-islam/translation/#p-365462).

[^6]: [*Manusmṛti*, ch. 2 (translation), [p-c52281](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/#p-c52281), [p-a9e644](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/#p-a9e644).
